author: felicepantaleo
body:
The v19 HGCal geometry introduces a fourth silicon sensor category
(HGCSiliconDetId type 3, HD 200um). Two places mishandle it on the default
(non-aging) simulation and reconstruction path; both are fixed here.

**1. Digitizer zero-suppression threshold (first commit).**
In the legacy branch of HGCDigitizerBase, cell.thickness (1..4) is used
directly as the MIP-charge scale of the ZS threshold. This proxy is valid
for 120/200/300um (types 0-2) but gives HD 200um a factor 4 instead of 2:
thrADC = 27 (2.64 fC), above the HD200 MIP MPV of 2.57 fC, i.e. a cut at
~1.26 MIP. Measured with single muons at eta 2.3-2.5 (D122 vs D121, no PU):
37% of sim hits in type-3 cells produced no rechit (62.9% efficiency vs
98-100% for all other types), with a three-fold phi pattern following the
HD-wafer placement. The fix maps the HD200 factor to 2. Rerunning the same
GEN events through the fixed digitizer restores type-3 efficiency to 98.8%.
At campaign level (1280-sample A/B on identical SIM), the digitization and
reco fixes together recover 55-70% of the pion merged-trackster energy
deficit across eta 1.9-2.5 and reduce the layer-cluster fragmentation peak
from +44% to +35%.

**2. v19 four-slot constants in local reco (second commit).**
The v19 thicknessCorrection has 8 silicon entries (4 per section) but
deltasi_index_regemfac remained 3, so all CE-H silicon rechits were
misbinned (CE-H HD120 read the CE-E HD200 slot: +12% energy and wrong
sigma-noise). The layer clusterers additionally copy their constants from
HGCalRecHit at import time, before era modifiers apply, so on v19 they ran
with pre-v19 6-slot arrays and sent CE-H HD200 hits to the scintillator
threshold slot. This adds deltasi_index_regemfac = 4 to the v19 rechit
modifier and explicit v19 overrides (8 thickness slots, v19 fCPerMIP,
noises, thickness corrections) for the EE/HSi/HSci cluster producers.

**3. Aging noise customisation v19 block (third commit).**
In HGCal_setRealisticNoiseSi the v19 toModify block had a duplicated
noise line, set HGCAL_chargeCollectionEfficiencies to the noise list
(endOfLifeNoises_v19) instead of endOfLifeCCEs_v19, and targeted the
module-level PSets rather than the ones just attached to the process, so
none of it took effect on a loaded process. Deduplicated, corrected and
retargeted; verified era-gated (v19 processes get the four-entry values,
others keep the defaults).

Validation: single-photon and single-muon A/B scans D121(v17) vs D122(v19)
in eta [1.3, 3.3] x full phi; muon sim-hit-to-rechit efficiency by cell
type before/after; photon energy closure unchanged within 1%. Build, unit
tests of the touched packages, code-checks and code-format are clean.

**4. v19 ToA jitter constant and per-type time offset (fourth commit).**
The v19 jitterConstant_ns was 0.2 ns, ten times the 0.02 ns default (a
copy of the adjacent eventTimeOffset_ns line), and generateTimeOffset
looped over a hardcoded 3 thickness classes, leaving the fourth v19 class
(HD 200um) a frozen +0.2 ns ToA bias instead of a random draw. The
coherent per-event HGCal-vs-MTD shift fails the timing compatibility in
TICL track-trackster linking: with single pions (pt 10) from the origin,
charged-candidate linking efficiency was 31-50% in v19 vs 94-98% in v17;
with this fix it returns to 94.2-98.8%, flat in eta. Related observation for the TICL
experts: the compatibility tolerance in GeneralInterpretationAlgo has no
floor or systematic term, so any inter-detector timing miscalibration
becomes a linking cliff rather than a resolution loss, and an unlinked
non-muon track currently produces no candidate at all.

**5. HLT twins of the offline v19 constants (fifth commit).**
A systematic sweep of the digitization and HLT configs found the same
defect classes in HLT_75e33: deltasi_index_regemfac stayed 3 in
hltHGCalRecHit(L1Seeded) and the HSi layer-cluster producers under v19
(CE-H HD120 rechits +12% energy; HLT CLUE thresholds off by -44% to +78%
per type), and the v19 pset override never replaced dEdXweights, leaving
the whole HLT HGCal energy scale on v16 absorber weights (up to ~10% per
layer in CE-E). All fixed with additions to the existing phase2_hgcalV19
modifiers; verified under the era that HLT layerWeights now equal the
offline dEdX_v19 list.

**6. Hardening and cleanup (sixth commit).**
generateTimeOffset now bounds its loop by both array sizes; the v19
toModify in HFNose_setRealisticNoiseSi targets the process attribute
(same defect as commit 3); stale thickness comments corrected.

Known issues NOT addressed here, left to the experts:
- HGCalSiNoiseMap indexes FOUR 3-entry containers with type() (also via
  setGeometry on the cached path), out of range for type 3 on the
  scaleByDose (aging) path; extending it needs validated HD200
  capacitance/volume/CCE parameters (a copied LD200 capacitance would be
  wrong for the half-area HD pad). The aging path is unusable for v19
  until then, and the v19 dose/fluence map itself predates the v19 layer
  stack.
- CLUE seeding sigmaNoise is read without the CE-H offset while the
  threshold is read with it (HGCalCLUEAlgo.cc:54-58, also the alpaka
  producer): a ~12% CE-H seeding asymmetry predating v19; needs an A/B
  measurement before changing.
- HGCal_disableNoise builds HGCAL_noise_heback without sipmMap and throws
  MissingParameter on use; debug path only.
title: Fix v19 HGCal ToA jitter constant and per-type time offset generation
